memoryArtifacts.headline: Bruce, Maud Matilda story 1282-1323
memoryArtifacts.description: When Maud Matilda Bruce was born in 1282, her father, Robert, was 39, and her mother, Margaret, was 28. She married Hugh mac Uilleam de Ross, Earl of Ross in 1308 in Ross, Kirkcudbrightshire, Scotland …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Matilda de Bruce於1287年出生在英國蘇格蘭艾斯爾吉爾可斯瓦爾德時，她的父親Robert de Brus 6th Lord of Annandale44歲，母親Lady Marjorie de Carrick 3rd Countess of Carrick33歲。她去世於1326年在英國蘇格蘭羅斯 安德 可洛馬迪費亞享年39歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德貝維其可-朋-特維德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Matilda de Bruce于1287年出生在英国苏格兰艾斯爾吉爾可斯瓦爾德时，她的父亲Robert de Brus 6th Lord of Annandale44岁，母亲Lady Marjorie de Carrick 3rd Countess of Carrick33岁。她去世于1326年在英国苏格兰羅斯 安德 可洛馬迪費亞享年39岁，埋葬在英国英格兰諾圖波藍德貝維其可-朋-特維德。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bruce
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (of Norman origin): habitational name from a place in Normandy, probably from Brix (La Manche).

History: The son of the Domesday baron, a friend of David I of Scotland, was granted by him the Lordship of Annandale in 1124, and his second son Robert became the founder of the Scottish house of Bruce. — This surname (see 2 above) is listed along with its original form Brousse in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors.

variants.name: Brousse
meanings: French: topographic name for someone living in a scrubby area, from Old French bro(u)ce ‘thicket, brushwood’ (from Late Latin bruscia), or a habitational name from any of several places in France named Brousse. Compare Bruce and Brusse .

History: This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors (along with its altered form Bruce) and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of South Carolina.

variants.name: Brewis
meanings: (of Norman origin): habitational name from Briouze in Orne.
(Northumberland and Durham): topographic name or nickname from Middle English breuhous ‘brewery’ (literally ‘brew house’).

nameMeanings.name: Matilda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

variants.name: Mathilda
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

en.lifeSummary: When Matilda de Bruce was born in 1287, in Kirkoswald, Ayrshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, her father, Robert de Brus 6th Lord of Annandale, was 44 and her mother, Lady Marjorie de Carrick 3rd Countess of Carrick, was 33. She died in 1326, in Fearn, Ross and Cromarty, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 39, and was buried in Berwick-upon-Tweed, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom.

fr.Fearn, Ross and Cromarty, Scotland: Fearn, Ross and Cromarty, Écosse, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Matilda de Bruce est née en 1287, à Kirkoswald, Ayrshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, Robert de Brus 6th Lord of Annandale, avait 44 ans et sa mère, Lady Marjorie de Carrick 3rd Countess of Carrick, avait 33 ans. Elle est décédée en 1326, à Fearn, Ross and Cromarty, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 39 ans, et a été enterrée à Berwick-upon-Tweed, Northumberland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Sie starb 1326 im Alter von 39 Jahren in Fearn, Ross and Cromarty, Schottland, United Kingdom und wurde in Berwick-upon-Tweed, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom begraben.

ko.Fearn, Ross and Cromarty, Scotland: 영국 스코틀랜드 로스 앤드 크로마티 피른
ko.lifeSummary: Matilda de Bruce(이)가 1287년 영국 스코틀랜드 에이르실르 커크즈월드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert de Brus 6th Lord of Annandale(은)는 44세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Marjorie de Carrick 3rd Countess of Carrick(은)는 33세였다. 그녀는 1326년 영국 스코틀랜드 로스 앤드 크로마티 피른에서 39세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노르스벌랜드 버윅-업-트위드에 묻혔다.

ru.Fearn, Ross and Cromarty, Scotland: фирн, росс анд кромерти, Шотландия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Matilda de Bruce родилась в 1287 г. в местности киркосвалд, аиршир, Шотландия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Robert de Brus 6th Lord of Annandale было 44 года, а матери, которую звали Lady Marjorie de Carrick 3rd Countess of Carrick, 33. Она умерла в 1326 г. в местности фирн, росс анд кромерти, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 39 лет и была похоронена в местности бирвикк-упон-твид, нортумбирланд, Англия, Великобритания.
